body.title: pe_chain auto-closes tickets with 'commit=skipped: no edit applied' — should escalate or block instead
body.result: Three-layer guard: pe_close_loop escalates on implement_skipped (existing) + commit_result.startswith('skipped') (new) + _pe_close defensive guard refuses when implement_skipped OR commit_skipped OR no implement_files (new). All paths to ticket-close now require evidence of real work shipped. WARNING log on refusal so next leak is debuggable. Test fixture _passing_basket fixed to set implement_files. 7 new tests, 4133 regression pass.
body.description:
Observed 2026-04-29: Igor's pe_chain ran on T-adc-installer-design-call (a design-call ticket auto-unblocked by T-cc-queue-gate-clear-on-close), found no plan_summary/no test_criterion/no plan_files/no hypothesis, ran existing tests (pass), then closed the ticket with 'pe_chain autonomous: pass' / 'commit=skipped: no edit applied'. Result: design ticket closed without producing design content.

Fix: pe_chain CLOSE step should refuse to close when commit=skipped AND no edits were applied AND ticket isn't trivially empty. Either escalate (block ticket with 'autonomous closure refused — needs human design'), or restore status=pending with a note. Don't ship empty closures as if they were real work.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (CLOSE step) — also check pe_chain_qwen_tier or successor module.
**Design rules:** approach-frame (positive: 'pe_chain confirms real work shipped before closing'). No-sqlite. Test-plan-or-why-not.
**Scope boundary:** in scope: refuse-to-close-when-empty in pe_chain CLOSE step. Out of scope: redesigning ticket-pickup heuristics, fixing past empty closures (T-adc-installer-design-call can be reopened manually if Akien wants the design).
**Test plan:** unit test pe_chain CLOSE rejects close when commit_status='skipped' AND edits_applied=0; passes through normally when edits applied. Live verify after deploy.
